title: Flag values cached at first read with no TTL — kill-switch toggles never take effect
product_summary: Acme storefront config service (Node/Express): serves feature flag values (promo banner, maintenance mode) fetched from a hosted flag provider

ground_truth.root_cause: createFlagCache() in src/flags.js fetches provider values only when fetchedAt === 0 (first read) and never again: getFlag() ignores the ttlMs option entirely, so flag toggles at the provider (e.g. disabling the promo banner kill-switch) only reach served values after a process restart. The provider and any CDN in front of /api/config are fine.
ground_truth.fix_contract: Served flag values reflect provider-side changes within at most 60 seconds: the cache honors its ttlMs option (default no higher than 60s) or re-reads per request. With an injected clock, toggling the provider value and advancing past the TTL changes the value returned by getFlag(); values fetched successfully keep being served for reads inside the TTL, and unknown flags still fall back.
expected_evidence: config_served events reporting promo_banner=true for hours after the provider-side toggle
flags_refreshed firing only a handful of times per day, lining up with process starts/deploys rather than any refresh interval
distractors: the hosted flag provider's API or CDN serving stale values
load balancer or CDN caching of /api/config responses
